data.title: Brazil's Central Bank Maintains Economic Growth Forecast Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Brazil's central bank has decided to uphold its economic growth forecast for the current year, despite the increased uncertainty caused by the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's commitment to its projections despite the geopolitical tensions. The central bank's decision reflects confidence in the country's economic resilience, even as global markets face volatility due to the situation in Iran. The bank's stance suggests a focus on domestic economic factors and stability, aiming to navigate through the external challenges posed by the conflict.

data.title: X Hires Former Aave and Base Design Lead for X Money Expansion
data.description: X has appointed Benji Taylor, previously the design lead at Aave and Base, as the head of design in preparation for the broader launch of X Money next month. According to NS3.AI, the payments product currently offers a 6% annual percentage yield (APY) on wallet cash balances. X Money is in a limited external beta phase and plans to introduce peer-to-peer payments, wallet services, and a Visa debit card linked to user accounts.

data.title: Nicolás Maduro Faces U.S. Court in Drug-Trafficking Case
data.description: Nicolás Maduro is scheduled to appear in a Manhattan federal court on Thursday as the United States advances its case against him involving drug-trafficking and narco-terrorism conspiracy charges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this legal proceeding. The case represents a major international legal effort by the U.S. to address allegations against the former Venezuelan leader. Maduro's court appearance is part of a broader strategy to combat drug-related activities linked to his regime.

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Align with Expectations
data.description: The number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending March 21 reached 210,000, meeting expectations. According to BlockBeats, this figure is consistent with the anticipated number and shows a slight increase from the previous week's count of 205,000.

data.title: Analysis: Escalating US-Iran tensions trigger Bitcoin decline; market uncertainty reaches new highs.
data.description: The US Department of Defense is preparing for a "deadly strike" against Iran, with the Pentagon having developed military plans including ground troops and large-scale bombing. Coupled with the expiration of Trump's pause on strikes against Iran on Friday, Bitcoin fell below $70,000 again, a 24-hour drop of approximately 3%. Glassnode stated that the short-term holding cost of Bitcoin at $70,200 is a key support level, but buying pressure is limited, and the risk of breaking below this level cannot be ignored. Tim Sun, a senior researcher at HashKey Group, believes that this level may be repeatedly tested, and the current rebound relies on leverage; a reversal in sentiment could easily lead to a price pullback. On the macro level, the volatility of VIX futures surged to 388.2 last month (a near six-month high), four times the average level of market fear. The Kobeissi Letter points out that the current global market uncertainty is unprecedented, further exacerbating the volatility risk of crypto assets. (Decrypt)

data.title: European Central Bank's Simkus Reappointed as Swiss National Bank President
data.description: The Swiss National Bank has announced the reappointment of European Central Bank Governing Council member Simkus as its president. According to Jin10, this decision underscores the continued collaboration between European financial institutions. Simkus's leadership is expected to maintain stability and foster growth within the Swiss financial sector. The reappointment reflects confidence in his ability to navigate the challenges facing the European and Swiss economies.

data.title: Hashdex Expands Crypto ETF to Include ADA and LINK
data.description: Hashdex has expanded its Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF (NCIQ) to include seven assets, according to its first annual SEC 10-K filing. According to Odaily, the ETF now comprises ADA and LINK, in addition to the existing BTC, ETH, XRP, SOL, and XLM. This expansion enhances the ETF's multi-asset allocation, covering more mainstream public chains and infrastructure tokens. The move aims to mitigate the volatility risk of single assets and reflects institutional investors' ongoing demand for diversified crypto asset exposure.

data.title: Sonic SVM Foundation Acquires ForgeX and Open Sources Core Product
data.description:
Sonic SVM Foundation has announced the acquisition of ForgeX, a developer of market-making tools on the Solana blockchain. According to Foresight News, the foundation has also open-sourced ForgeX's core product, ForgeX CLI.

ForgeX CLI is designed for Solana developers, project teams, and AI agents, offering a suite of on-chain operational tools. It supports token creation across multiple launchpads, atomic developer purchases, same-block transactions, multi-wallet collaboration, volume management, and cross-DEX on-chain market-making. All commands natively output structured JSON, enabling direct execution by AI agents.

data.title: Japan Considers Oil Market Intervention to Bolster Yen
data.description:
Japan's government has indicated potential intervention in the oil market as a strategy to support the weakening yen. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's concerns over the currency's depreciation and its impact on the economy. The yen has been under pressure due to various global economic factors, prompting officials to explore measures that could stabilize its value.

The move comes amid broader efforts by Japan to address economic challenges, including inflation and trade imbalances. By intervening in the oil market, the government aims to influence currency dynamics indirectly, potentially providing relief to sectors affected by the yen's decline.

This approach reflects Japan's ongoing commitment to maintaining economic stability and competitiveness in the face of fluctuating global markets. The government's actions are closely watched by investors and analysts, who are assessing the potential implications for international trade and financial markets.

data.title: Fitch: Rising Oil Prices to Impact Economic Growth in South Korea, Japan, and the U.S.
data.description: Fitch Ratings has indicated that the recent surge in oil prices is expected to significantly affect the economic growth of South Korea, Japan, and the United States. According to Jin10, the increase in oil prices could lead to higher production costs and inflationary pressures in these countries, potentially slowing down their economic recovery. The report highlights that these nations, being major importers of oil, are particularly vulnerable to fluctuations in oil prices. As a result, the economic outlook for these countries may face challenges if the upward trend in oil prices continues.

data.title: STOCKS | Fitch: Stock Market Decline Hits Canada, South Korea, and U.S. Hardest
data.description: Fitch Ratings has identified Canada, South Korea, and the United States as the countries most affected by the recent downturn in the stock market. According to Jin10, the credit rating agency highlighted that these nations are experiencing significant economic impacts due to the market's volatility. The analysis suggests that the decline in stock values is exerting pressure on financial systems and investor confidence in these regions. Fitch's report underscores the need for careful monitoring of market trends and potential policy responses to mitigate further economic disruptions.

data.title: UK Government Reactivates Ethanol Plant Amid CO2 Shortage Concerns
data.description: The UK government has temporarily reactivated an ethanol plant due to concerns over a potential carbon dioxide shortage linked to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of carbon dioxide as a crucial byproduct in food production and healthcare sectors. The decision underscores the government's proactive measures to mitigate supply chain disruptions and ensure the availability of essential resources.

data.title: Nvidia-backed Reflection AI raises its funding target to $2.5 billion, valuing the company at $25 billion.
data.description: Reflection, an AI startup backed by Nvidia, has raised its funding target to $2.5 billion (previously $2 billion), potentially valuing the company at $25 billion (previously $20 billion). If successful, this would be one of the largest funding rounds in the open-source AI space. Founded in 2024 by former Google DeepMind researchers Misha Laskin and Ioannis Antonoglou, and headquartered in New York, the company focuses on building a freely usable and customizable US-based open-source AI model system, aiming to create a technology network on the Nvidia chip ecosystem to rival overseas competitors. Sources indicate that JPMorgan Chase plans to participate in this round through its "Security and Resilience Program," and existing investor Disruptive is also expected to participate. (Techstartups)

data.title: Analysis: Investors are repeating the "abandoning stocks and bonds, hoarding cash" strategy seen in 2022, indicating a rise in risk aversion.
data.description: To mitigate the risks of a potential war with Iran, investors are selling off stocks and bonds and increasing their cash holdings, a strategy mirroring that employed by Russia after its 2022 invasion of Ukraine. A recent Bank of America survey of fund managers this month showed the largest jump in cash holdings in six years. Meanwhile, JPMorgan strategists said this week that the portfolio adjustments made in response to the conflict may be far from over. A report from the JPMorgan team, led by Nicholas Panigizoglou, stated that as long as geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty remains high, current below-historic cash allocations will negatively impact future stock and bond markets. Investors have been simultaneously selling stocks, bonds, and gold, favoring cash holdings. However, compared to the levels seen at the outbreak of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, cash levels in portfolios remain moderate. (Jinshi)

data.title: Investors Reassess Private Credit Amid Challenges
data.description: Investors are showing a growing readiness to withdraw funds from private credit investments as the sector encounters significant challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shifting sentiment among investors who are reconsidering their positions in these once-niche funds. The private credit market, which has seen substantial growth in recent years, is now facing headwinds that are prompting stakeholders to reevaluate their strategies. This trend reflects broader concerns about the stability and future prospects of private credit as an investment vehicle. As the market dynamics evolve, investors are closely monitoring developments to make informed decisions about their portfolios.

data.title: Brazil Enacts Law to Integrate Seized Crypto Assets into Public Security Funds
data.description:
Brazilian President Lula has signed Law No. 15,358, which incorporates seized crypto assets into the public security funding system for expenses such as police equipment, intelligence operations, and personnel training. According to Odaily, the law permits the temporary use of these assets before final conviction, subject to court approval.

The new regulation significantly expands judicial powers, allowing authorities to freeze, block, or confiscate crypto assets during investigations. This includes restricting access to exchange accounts, digital wallets, and related platforms. Upon conviction, individuals involved will be permanently barred from using the formal financial and crypto systems.

Additionally, the law classifies the use of encrypted communication tools or privacy technologies to conceal criminal activities as an aggravating factor. It also promotes international asset recovery and intelligence sharing, while establishing a national database to integrate the financial structures of criminal organizations.

Analysts believe this move signifies Brazil's shift from viewing crypto assets as potential reserve tools to enforcement resources, strengthening efforts against organized crime, such as PCC and Comando Vermelho. It also advances the judicial system's ability to regulate and manage digital assets.

data.title: Canadian Foreign Minister Advocates for De-escalation in Iran Conflict
data.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand announced her intention to urge G-7 leaders to work towards reducing hostilities in the ongoing conflict involving the U.S. and Israel in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this call comes amid a global struggle with rising energy prices. Anand's appeal reflects growing international concern over the economic impact of the conflict.

data.title: A Reuters poll showed that most economists believe the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates 1-2 times this year.
data.description: According to a Reuters poll, 65 out of 82 economists (approximately 79%) believe the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates one or two times this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Faces Worst Month in a Year Amid Declines
data.description: The S&P 500 Index has experienced four consecutive weeks of declines, positioning it for its worst monthly performance in a year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by the index. Investors are closely monitoring economic indicators and market trends as they navigate this period of uncertainty. The persistent downturn has raised concerns about the broader implications for the financial markets.

data.title: Bittensor Emerges as Top Performer Among Altcoins
data.description: Research firm 10x Research recently highlighted Bittensor (TAO) as one of the standout altcoins over the past two weeks. According to Odaily, Bittensor has shown remarkable performance within the firm's 50+ asset chart library. As Bitcoin's market dominance slightly declines, altcoins are exhibiting early bullish signals. However, overall market momentum remains weak, with trading volumes relatively subdued. Many altcoins are gradually developing more attractive technical patterns, and their fundamentals are improving. In the current environment, successful investment relies more on selecting specific assets rather than broad allocation, making the identification of potential winners crucial.
